items.title: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
items.description: Despite President Donald Trump's refusal to sign Congress' bipartisan housing bill, it's set to go into effect at midnight, and its temporary CBDC ban along with it.

items.title: Hyundai becomes first major South Korean company to introduce internal stablecoin transfers
items.description: The initiative builds on a broader shift by companies exploring stablecoins to move money between international operations more efficiently.

items.title: OKX, MetaMask, Matter Labs back dispute resolution court for AI agents
items.description: The Genlayer Foundation is leading the 27-firm consortium that makes AI-based payments, escrow and dispute resolution interoperable.

items.title: Circle soars after securing U.S. trust bank approval in crypto expansion
items.description: The approval adds to a growing list of crypto firms seeking federal banking licenses as the industry moves into the regulated financial system.

items.title: Japan's 'invest locally' plan likely to spur demand for assets like bitcoin, gold
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 10, 2026

items.title: Crypto defies equity weakness as altcoin optimism builds into the weekend
items.description: The price of bitcoin hit $64,400, retesting a level it failed to penetrate on Monday, with a break higher opening the path toward the June 15 peak of $67,250.

items.title: Polymarket seeks approval to bring margin trading to U.S. customers
items.description: Polymarket's application to allow users to take positions that are not fully collateralized follows authorization granted to rival Kalshi in March.

items.title: Bitcoin's $60,000-$70,000 range becomes third most traded range in history
items.description: Bitcoin has spent 307 days in the $60,000-$70,000 range, the third longest consolidation in any $10,000 price band.

items.title: New Hampshire council votes down $100M Bitcoin bonds
items.description: State representative Keith Ammon said the New Hampshire executive council's vote against Bitcoin-backed bonds was “short-sighted,” urging the body to reconsider the measure.

items.title: Backpack joins race for 24/7 stock markets with tokenized equities
items.description: The launch comes as tokenized stocks become one of the fastest-growing corners of crypto, with exchanges and traditional finance firms racing to expand 24/7 trading.

items.title: Crypto Biz: How stablecoins found their niche
items.description: Stablecoins are carving out specialized roles as regulation reshapes the market, while Strategy’s Bitcoin sale and Vanguard’s tokenization push highlight crypto’s evolving financial landscape.

items.title: Bitcoin whales sent BTC price to $64K as Coinbase Premium broke key level: CryptoQuant
items.description: Bitcoin price gains came thanks to US whales propeling the Coinbase Premium above a key trend line, new analysis from CryptoQuant reported.

items.title: A16z’s Andreessen lands Federal Reserve role as AI reshapes policy debate
items.description: The Fed named a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen to co-lead an AI productivity and jobs task force under Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy review.

items.title: MiCA licensing only the beginning as crypto custodians face scrutiny
items.description: A MiCA license allows crypto firms to operate in the EU, but the ESMA’s review will test whether custodians can meet the required security and resilience standards.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Will the crypto lobby's $189M campaign get CLARITY over the line?
items.description: As the CLARITY Act advances, crypto's growing political influence in Washington is under scrutiny. But the crypto lobby's massive campaign spend is only part of the story.

items.title: SK Hynix opens at $170 on Nasdaq. Chairman tells CNBC 'demand is enormous'
items.description: SK Hynix has soared to a trillion-dollar market cap by serving some of the biggest names in technology, including Nvidia and Apple.

items.title: Trump says he won't sign housing bill, which would become law automatically
items.description: Congress passed the housing bill in June with strong bipartisan support amid concerns of rising home prices and institutional investors affecting the market.

items.title: Stablecoin issuer Circle just got the greenlight to operate as a bank. The shares are up 5%
items.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle surged in premarket trading after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted it approval to operate as a trust bank.

items.title: Traders fall back in love with Meta. Here's where bulls see it going
items.description: Meta Platform's AI efforts are looking like the recipe for a comeback after an almost year-long drought in shares of the $1.7 trillion market-cap company.

items.title: Senate Democrats want hearings on Trump’s crypto holdings and foreign investors
items.description: Democratic Senators cited $1.4 billion in Trump family crypto income, unidentified investors and a reported UAE-backed stake in World Liberty Financial.

items.title: As bank earnings approach, a market anomaly emerges
items.description: The Financial Select Sector Index trades at roughly 15.5 times forward earnings — about a turn and a quarter cheaper than where it stood in 2024.

items.title: Trump purges Election Assistance Commission members, months before midterms
items.description: The White House said the Supreme Court's ruling allowing President Trump to fire FTC Commissioner Louise Slaughter provided the precedent for the removals.

items.title: These are America's 10 most expensive states for 2026, where inflation is punishing residents
items.description: These 10 U.S. states are the most expensive places to live in America in 2026, with inflation punishing residents to an extreme degree.

- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cVB5Uy_OoZI! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Man, 26, arrested on suspicion of murder after death of former Tory minister Ann Widdecombe
items.description: Devon and Cornwall Police say Widdecombe, 78, sustained ‘serious injuries’

items.title: UK to sign up to €60bn EU defence loan scheme for Ukraine
items.description: Keir Starmer keen to leave Downing Street with positive signal to Europe by pledging financial commitment

items.title: ‘It’s all about the number’: how Apollo gazumped Castlelake’s easyJet bid
items.description: Takeover battle for UK budget airline took a last-minute twist after US investment giant outbid rival

items.title: SK Hynix’s US shares jump 14% on Nasdaq debut
items.description: South Korean memory chipmaker’s US-listed stock price rose as high as $175 after pricing at $149

items.title: Investors sell longer-dated AI debt amid Big Tech borrowing spree
items.description: Waning demand highlights scepticism over sector’s long-term profitability

items.title: Trump says US has agreed to continue talks with Iran
items.description: President declares that ceasefire is ‘over’ despite ongoing negotiations

items.title: Mass immigration is not the silver bullet economists think it is
items.description: As with the free-trade debate in recent years, consensus is shifting

items.title: Nato’s carnival of Trumpian caprice
items.description: Alliance summits have become an exercise in managing an erratic US president

items.title: Shippers Face Deepening Dilemma as U.S. and Iran Vie for Control of the Strait
items.description: Companies desperately want to get their ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz, but face mounting risks. “Things are becoming uglier by the minute,” one executive said.

items.title: Atlassian Illegally Fired Worker in Rare Legal Victory for Tech Worker
items.description: A federal labor law judge determined last week that the software maker Atlassian had illegally fired an employee who questioned company policy changes.

items.title: A.I. Has Rewarded Investors. It May Now Pose Their Greatest Risk.
items.description: Near the midpoint of the year, stocks and bonds both report good returns. But the global stock market has become highly concentrated, our columnist says.

items.title: How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China
items.description: The German auto giant’s push into China powered it for decades, but now the company faces fierce competition from Chinese automakers in markets around the world.

items.title: The Work of Helping A.I. Destroy Work
items.description: Start-ups are paying white-collar professionals to teach their jobs to artificial intelligence models. It’s a bonanza. It’s bleak. Where will it end?

items.title: Wall St. Sets Limits on Prediction Market Trading
items.description: Goldman Sachs is the latest firm to restrict how employees use the popular betting platforms.

items.title: Meta Ordered by E.U. to Alter ‘Addictive Design’ of Instagram and Facebook
items.description: European Union authorities said the company’s use of “addictive design” violated a digital safety law.

items.title: You’re Freezing in Your Office Building. Here’s Why.
items.description: Office temperatures are typically set in the low 70s during summer. But where you sit and the efficiency of the cooling system can make you feel colder.
